metadata.title: Build a Handoff Condition Writer with Codex

then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Tests pass is not a handoff condition — it misses the three most common failures that tests never see.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Tests pass is not a handoff condition — it misses the three most common failures that tests never see.

beats.narration_text: A handoff condition must be specific — names a file, function, count, or value, not a vibe. Testable — produces a definite answer when run. And binary — pass or fail, no 'mostly'. The dangerous middle — code that looks complete and is functionally broken — passes 'tests pass' and fails all three handoff properties.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A handoff condition must be specific — names a file, function, count, or value, not a vibe. Testable — produces a definite answer when run. And binary — pass or

beats.narration_text: claude "Write a handoff condition for this Codex build step. Must be: specific, testable, binary. Include: file that must exist, function signature that must match, test cases that must pass, and one must-not condition for dangerous middle failure." < step_description.txt

props.command: claude "Write a handoff condition for this build step. Each element must be: (1) Specific, (2) Testable, (3) Binary. Include a must-not condition." < step_description.txt

build.suggested: suggested prompt: claude "Write a handoff condition for this Codex build step. Must be: specific, testable, binary. Include: file that must exist, function signature that must ma

beats.narration_text: handoff_writer.py — generates condition from step description, validates each element against specificity, testability, and binary rubric, flags any element that fails.

props.code:
# generates handoff condition and validates each element
import handoff_engine

step = open('step_description.txt').read()
condition = handoff_engine.generate(step)
for element in condition.elements:
    rubric = handoff_engine.validate(element)
    print(f'{element.name}: SPECIFIC={rubric.specific} TESTABLE={rubric.testable} BINARY={rubric.binary}')
    if not rubric.passes:
        print(f'  FLAG: {rubric.failure_reason}')

beats.narration_text: Generated condition for authentication step: (1) auth.py exists at project root — SPECIFIC, TESTABLE, BINARY, all pass. (2) authenticate signature present — all three pass. (3) pytest tests/test_auth.py passes — all three pass. (4) MUST NOT: no global session variable — all three pass. Condition complete: all four elements pass the rubric.

beats.narration_text: Generate handoff condition for a refactoring step; show the must-not adapts to that step type.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Generate handoff condition for a refactoring step; show the must-not adapts to that step type.

beats.narration_text: Refactoring step condition: the must-not element changes to 'no method renamed without corresponding test rename' — a different dangerous middle: wrong signature after rename, all tests still pass because they test the old name. The condition adapts to the step type because the must-not element is generated from the step's specific failure modes.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Refactoring step condition: the must-not element changes to 'no method renamed without corresponding test rename' — a different dangerous middle: wrong signatur

beats.narration_text: The handoff condition is the build's quality gate — not 'is this done?' but 'is this what step N was supposed to produce?' The must-not element is the most valuable: it names the failure that would slip through tests, which is the only failure that matters after 'tests pass'.
